Tests the behavior of the `arguments` object in a function when no arguments are passed.

# Purpose
This code is a simple JavaScript script that demonstrates the use of the `arguments` object within a function. The function [`f_arg`](<#04-004f_arg>) takes two parameters, `x` and `y`, but it does not use them directly. Instead, it returns the `arguments` object, which is an array-like object containing all the arguments passed to the function. The script includes an `assert` statement that checks if the first element of the `arguments` object returned by [`f_arg`](<#04-004f_arg>) is `undefined`, which occurs when the function is called without any arguments. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on illustrating the behavior of the `arguments` object in JavaScript functions.

### f\_arg<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/11/11.02/11.02.04/11.02.04-004.f_arg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/11/11.02/11.02.04/11.02.04-004.js#L17>)

Returns the `arguments` object containing all arguments passed to the function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `x`: The first argument passed to the function.
    - `y`: The second argument passed to the function.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Returns the `arguments` object which is an array-like object containing all arguments passed to the function.
- **Output**: The `arguments` object containing all arguments passed to the function.
